explanation: The property states that globally (G), if a switch to PSV mode is initiated by the user (PSVModeSelected), then it must eventually (F) be followed by the system asking for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters (confirmPSVParameters). This directly captures the 'when A then B' requirement of the natural language statement.

The natural language statement implies a reactive property: whenever a PCV-to-PSV switch is initiated, the system must eventually prompt for PSV parameter confirmation. The atomic proposition 'PSVModeSelected' captures the initiation of the switch by the user. The atomic proposition 'confirmPSVParameters' captures the system asking for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters. The G (globally) operator ensures that this condition holds at all times. The -> (implication) operator states that if 'PSVModeSelected' occurs, then F (eventually) 'confirmPSVParameters' must hold in the future. Therefore, the LTL formula 'G (PSVModeSelected -> F confirmPSVParameters)' accurately captures the required behavior.

The property states that globally (G), whenever a switch to PSV mode is initiated by the user (PSVModeSelected), it must eventually (F) be followed by the system asking for confirmation or setting of PSV parameters (confirmPSVParameters). This captures the 'When A, then B shall happen' structure of the natural language statement.

The property states that globally (G), whenever a PCV-to-PSV switch is initiated by the user (PSVModeSelected), it must eventually be the case that the system asks for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters (confirmPSVParameters). This directly captures the 'When A then eventually B' nature of the requirement.

The property states that globally (G), whenever a switch to PSV mode is initiated by the user (PSVModeSelected) while the system is in PCV mode (PCVMode), it must eventually (F) ask the user for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters (confirmPSVParameters). This captures the requirement that the system responds to the specific user-initiated mode change by prompting for parameter confirmation.

The property states that globally, whenever a switch to PSV mode is initiated by the user ('PSVModeSelected'), it must eventually be the case that the system asks for confirmation or setting of PSV parameters ('confirmPSVParameters'). This captures the 'When A, B shall happen' requirement, ensuring the confirmation step always follows the user's initiation of the PSV mode switch.

The property states that globally (G), whenever a switch to PSV mode is initiated by the user (PSVModeSelected), it must eventually (F) be followed by the confirmation of PSV parameters (confirmPSVParameters). This captures the 'When A, B shall happen' requirement, ensuring that the system asks for confirmation after the switch is initiated.

The natural language statement describes a conditional requirement: when a specific event occurs, another action must follow. The phrase "When a PCV-to-PSV switch is initiated by the user" is translated to the atomic proposition "PSVModeSelected", which indicates this user action. The consequence, "the system shall ask the user for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters to be used", is translated to "confirmPSVParameters". The LTL formula uses the "G" (Globally) operator to ensure that this property holds at all times. The implication "->" (implies) connects the trigger to the consequence. The "X" (Next) operator is used to indicate that the system's action of asking for confirmation must occur in the very next state after the user initiates the switch. This captures the immediate and mandatory nature implied by "shall ask" in the context of a system's response to a user input.

The property states that globally (G), if a switch to PSV mode is initiated by the user (PSVModeSelected), then it must eventually (F) be followed by the confirmation of PSV parameters (confirmPSVParameters). This directly captures the 'When A, B shall happen' structure of the natural language statement.

The LTL property states that globally, whenever a switch to PSV mode is initiated by the user ('PSVModeSelected'), it must eventually be the case that the system asks for confirmation or setting of PSV parameters ('confirmPSVParameters'). The 'G' (globally) operator ensures this condition holds at all times, and 'F' (eventually) ensures that the confirmation/setting action occurs at some point after the initiation.
